rankings.title: Is AI About to “Eat Everything”? (It’s Not.)
summary_bullets: METR chart measures AI coding task difficulty, not general intelligence.
AI coding gains come from post-training and hand-coded harnesses.
Progress in coding does not imply AI will "eat everything".
AI companies should distance from transhumanist hype.
rankings.display_title: Is AI About to “Eat Everything”? (It’s Not.)

rankings.title: Can This Simple Change Save My Distracted Brain?
summary_bullets: Two-week mobile internet block improves attention.
Study: blocking internet on phone cuts screen time by half.
Four mediators: offline activities, social interaction, sleep, self-control.
Only 25.5% of participants remained compliant.
Advice: block precisely, strengthen controls, lean into boredom.
rankings.display_title: Can This Simple Change Save My Distracted Brain?

rankings.title: Are We About to Lose Control of AI? (*sighs*)
summary_bullets: Anthropic report warns of recursive self-improvement.
Cal Newport argues fears are overblown.
Faster coding does not equal smarter AI.
Coding harnesses are controllable, not black boxes.
AI coding tools increase output but not innovation.
rankings.display_title: Are We About to Lose Control of AI? (*sighs*)

rankings.title: Has AI Conquered Coding? (It’s Not So Simple…)
summary_bullets: AI coding agents cause skill atrophy in developers.
Senior devs lose ability to review generated code.
Junior devs never learn to code without AI.
Fay proposes demoting AI to secondary role.
Developers should write code themselves for important tasks.
rankings.display_title: Has AI Conquered Coding? (It’s Not So Simple…)

rankings.title: Is the AI Doom Fever Breaking? (It’s About Time!)
summary_bullets: AI CEOs shift from apocalyptic rhetoric.
Cal Newport analyzes cultural roots in rationalism.
X-risk community influenced AI company culture.
Public opinion turning against AI companies.
Wall Street pressure may moderate CEO claims.
rankings.display_title: Is the AI Doom Fever Breaking? (It’s About Time!)

rankings.title: How Do I Fix My Motivation System?
summary_bullets: Digital addiction shares brain mechanisms with substance addiction.
Four C's: control, compulsion, craving, consequences.
Teenagers especially vulnerable due to developing brains.
Self-binding strategies and 12-step groups like ITAA help.
Parents should delay smartphones until at least age 16.
rankings.display_title: Digital Addiction: Neuroscience, Warning Signs, and Recovery Strategies

rankings.title: Was the Mythos Ban Justified? (Good Idea. Bad Execution.)
summary_bullets: Anthropic's Mythos model banned by US government.
Cal argues Mythos is not revolutionary for bug finding.
Guardrails on Fable 5 are easily jailbroken.
Government should require pre-release safety reviews.
AI companies' scare tactics cause psychological harm.
rankings.display_title: Was the Mythos Ban Justified? (Good Idea. Bad Execution.)

rankings.title: Did AI Just “Solve” Math? (Let’s Take a Closer Look)
summary_bullets: OpenAI claimed LLM disproved Erdos's 80-year-old conjecture.
Humans extracted idea from LLM's long chain-of-thought.
Result is important but not a sign of superhuman AI.
AI math tools are narrow, not general intelligence.
Cal argues AI should be treated as normal technology.
rankings.display_title: Did AI Just “Solve” Math? (Let’s Take a Closer Look)

rankings.title: How Do I Stop Wasting Time?
summary_bullets: Time scarcity is often a mindset issue.
People have more free time than they think.
Weekly planning and house rules reduce chaos.
Small steps enable big goals over time.
Intentional leisure crowds out low-quality screen time.
rankings.display_title: How Do I Stop Wasting Time?

rankings.title: How Do I Become AI-Proof?
summary_bullets: AI may make knowledge work miserable, not replace jobs.
Pseudo-productivity (visible busyness) is the root problem.
Digital tech and AI accelerate busyness into a "busyness singularity."
Five tactics: weekly planning, portfolio, avoid AI tasks, upskill, write well.
Managers can use transparent workloads and docket clearing.

rankings.title: Are You Too Busy to Think? | The Case for Pressing Pause
summary_bullets: Pressing pause boosts cognitive capacity and insight.
Three benefits: less context shifting, novel environments, distance from present.
Four levels of pause: coffee shop loop to full escape.
Systematic journaling: gratitude, stuck areas, radical to practical solutions.
Also answers listener questions on email, reading, and thinking.
rankings.display_title: Are You Too Busy to Think? | The Case for Pressing Pause

rankings.title: Are You Lazy or Just Overstimulated?
summary_bullets: Overstimulation ≠ laziness; it's context-switching fatigue.
Dopamine downregulation reduces ability to focus.
Quick fixes (e.g., morning detox) are insufficient.
Permanent reduction of stimulation is the real solution.
rankings.display_title: Are You Lazy or Just Overstimulated?

rankings.title: How to Be a Digital Minimalist in 2026
summary_bullets: Reviews six digital minimalism strategies from Reddit.
Grades each strategy (A, B, C) with reasoning.
Proposes three missing chapters for updated book.
Includes listener questions and baseball movie critique.
rankings.display_title: How to Be a Digital Minimalist in 2026

rankings.title: Do I Need a “Brain Gym”?
summary_bullets: Proposes tiered cognitive fitness system
Moderate: read, write, thinking walks, etc.
Serious: immersive thinking, point system
Predicts future industry: trainers, courses, endurance tests

rankings.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the “Doom Trolling”
summary_bullets: AI companies use fear-mongering about existential risks.
Cal Newport calls this “doom trolling” morally indefensible.
He argues companies either believe risks or are cynical.
Suggests ignoring future AI predictions, focus on current products.
Encourages using term “doom trolling” to undermine strategy.
rankings.display_title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the “Doom Trolling”
